target_game.name: Mob Control
target_game.core_loop: Player aims a cannon to shoot units through multiplier gates to grow a massive army, defeats an enemy base, earns rewards, and uses them to upgrade their own base and unit cards.
target_game.audience_proxy: Male-skewing casual gamers aged 18-35 who enjoy satisfying, short-session strategy games.

palette.description: A vibrant and high-contrast palette with a dominant royal purple, complemented by a bright cyan and a warm yellow accent, creating an energetic and engaging feel.

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · live-action-UGC

centroid_hook.summary: A young man dances energetically in a dimly lit room, expressing his joy after playing a mobile game.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A young man waves his arms above his head and dances to a beat in a room with blue ambient lighting.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: how I feel after playing Crossword Go all night after school

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 10d. Hook representative: "A young man dances energetically in a dimly lit room, expressing his joy after p"

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · animation

centroid_hook.summary: The video opens with the iconic character Goku in his Super Saiyan 3 form, yelling and charging forward to deliver his signature 'Dragon Fist' attack.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A close-up on an angry Super Saiyan 3 Goku, who then clenches his fist and launches towards the screen.

top_archetypes.rationale: 2 creatives across 2 unique advertisers, average age 434d. Hook representative: "The video opens with the iconic character Goku in his Super Saiyan 3 form, yelli"

game_fit_scores.rationale: Major friction: this is a live-action UGC reaction clip referencing a word/puzzle game ("Crossword Go"), with a muted blue/beige palette and zero gameplay — none of it conveys Mob Control's signature mob-growth, multiplier gates, or vibrant purple/cyan/yellow stylized 3D look that drives IPM in this genre. The dancing-satisfaction hook also skews older/casual-puzzle audience rather than the 18-35 male hyper-casual action player. Only run as a low-budget test if the team wants to explore UGC framing, and only if reshot with on-screen Mob Control gameplay B-roll and a hook tied to "satisfying crowd-multiplier" moments — otherwise skip.

This is a food-themed word/crossword satisfaction hook — fundamentally mismatched to Mob Control's aim-shoot-multiplier-army loop; there's no honest way to bridge a "spell DONUT" puzzle into cannon/mob gameplay without misleading creative that will tank D1 retention and inflate refunds. Palette (browns/yellows/greens) and casual word-game audience (skews older/female) also conflict with the purple/cyan energetic palette and 18-35 male-skewing action audience. Recommend skipping; if testing satisfaction hooks, source ones built around crowd-collision or gate-multiplier ASMR instead.

Friction is severe across the board: a calm finger-on-jigsaw hook contradicts Mob Control's energetic aim-and-shoot, mob-multiplier loop and would mislead installs into a hard bounce. The muted grey/green/blue member palette also clashes with the game's high-contrast royal purple + yellow signature, and the puzzle archetype skews older/female-leaning casual — opposite of the 18-35 male-skewing action audience. Do not adapt this archetype directly; if testing satisfaction beats, port only the 'oddly satisfying' emotional pitch into a gates-and-crowd-growth visual instead.

Hard pass for Mob Control. The ASMR spice-jar hook lives in a soft pastel pink/cream palette and a calm, sensory-satisfaction emotional register that directly conflicts with the game's high-contrast purple/cyan/yellow energetic competitive tone — there's no character, no crowd, no aiming cue, and nothing that telegraphs the multiplier-gate core loop in the first 3 seconds. Audience also skews female/lifestyle ASMR viewers rather than the 18-35 male casual-strategy player, and with only 1 creative from 1 advertiser at 143d freshness there's no velocity signal worth chasing. Don't adapt.

Hard pass for production: this archetype leans on licensed Dragon Ball Z IP (Goku, Super Saiyan 3, "Dragon Fist") which is a legal non-starter and also misrepresents Mob Control's gameplay — there's no 1v1 anime brawler loop, no aiming/multiplier/mob-growth payoff in the hook. Palette (yellow/orange/black) clashes with the royal purple + cyan brand system, and while the male 18-35 anime-fan audience does overlap demographically, the bait-and-switch risk tanks IPM and post-install retention. Only borrow the abstract takeaway — a loud, high-energy "power-up and charge" beat — and rebuild it natively around a giant mob bursting through a x10 gate.

brief.title: POV: My Mob Just Hit x512
brief.hook_3s: Phone glow on a hyped face — then BOOM, a purple tidal wave of stickmen erupts through a x256 gate.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Live-action UGC — young guy on couch in dim room lit by purple/cyan phone glow, eyes wide, mouthing 'no way' as overlay reads the hook.
3-7s: Hard cut to phone screen close-up — Mob Control gameplay: cannon fires tiny mob, slams through stacked x2, x4, x8 multiplier gates, crowd explodes into hundreds.
7-11s: Cut back to player throwing arms up, dancing in the cyan/purple ambient light, vibing to the beat as the on-screen mob steamrolls an enemy base in split-screen.
11-14s: Reward burst — yellow coin shower on phone, player kisses fingers chef's-kiss style, base upgrade animation pops.
14-15s: End card: Mob Control logo, massive purple mob silhouette, CTA button pulsing.
brief.visual_direction: Authentic vertical UGC selfie-cam framing for the live-action beats, room lit ONLY by RGB strip lights in #5824F5 royal purple and #00A3FF cyan to mirror the game's signature palette and bridge reality to gameplay. Phone-screen inserts use the native Stylized 3D Cartoon look — chunky cel-shaded stickmen, glossy multiplier gates, #FFD12C yellow coin and number pops for satisfying punch. High contrast, snappy whip-cut transitions synced to a trending bass-drop beat; energetic/competitive UI mood throughout. Color grade pushes purple shadows and cyan highlights so live-action and gameplay feel like one continuous world.
text_overlays: how I feel after one round of Mob Control 😮‍💨
brief.rationale: Adapts the winning satisfaction-UGC framing to Mob Control's 18-35 male hyper-casual audience by anchoring the emotional payoff to the game's signature mob-multiplier dopamine moment instead of a generic dance reaction. Live-action authenticity drives thumb-stop and CTR while embedded gameplay B-roll in the brand palette rescues the low visual/mechanic fit scores flagged in the scan. Run as a low-budget UGC test cell against a gameplay-only control; scale only if IPM beats baseline within 48h.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, Stylized 3D Cartoon style: a young man in a hoodie sitting on a couch in a dim bedroom, face lit by phone glow, mouth open in shocked excitement, RGB strip lights casting royal purple #5824F5 and bright cyan #00A3FF across the walls, warm yellow #FFD12C phone screen reflection on his face, on-screen text overlay reading 'x256 MOB UNLOCKED' in bold chunky yellow caps, vertical mobile ad composition, high contrast, energetic mood, cinematic depth of field

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Stylized 3D Cartoon style: over-the-shoulder shot of a smartphone screen showing a massive cel-shaded purple stickman crowd flooding through a glowing multiplier gate labeled 'x8', cannon at the bottom, enemy red base in the distance, palette dominated by royal purple #5824F5 and cyan #00A3FF with #FFD12C yellow gate numbers and coin sparks, on-screen text 'watch the multiplier hit…' at top, glossy chunky 3D render, vibrant hyper-casual game UI

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Stylized 3D Cartoon style: split composition with live-action young man on left throwing arms up in victory under purple #5824F5 and cyan #00A3FF ambient lighting, and a 3D cartoon Mob Control gameplay shot on right with a giant yellow #FFD12C coin shower exploding over a defeated enemy base, on-screen text 'ok I'm addicted' in bold white caps with yellow stroke, energetic competitive vibe, vertical mobile ad framing

brief.title: Charge the x10: Mob Unleashed
brief.hook_3s: Tiny mob screams, cannon glows cyan-purple, then BOOM — they rocket toward a giant golden x10 gate.
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Close-up on a tiny, outnumbered purple mob trembling in front of a glowing cannon; camera shake, cyan energy charges up, yellow 'x10' gate looms ahead.

3-7s POWER-UP BEAT: Player finger drags to aim, cannon fires — slow-mo burst of stick-figures streaming through the x10 gate, multiplying into a massive roaring crowd as numbers count up 10 → 100 → 1000.
7-11s PAYOFF: The now-enormous purple mob floods the enemy's cyan base, smashing towers in a satisfying cartoon crunch; yellow coins and confetti explode upward.
11-14s PROGRESSION TEASE: Quick cut to base-upgrade screen — unit card levels up with a golden flash, hinting at the meta-loop.
14-15s CTA CARD: Logo slam with cannon icon and 'Play Free' button pulsing.
brief.visual_direction: Stylized 3D Cartoon with chunky, readable silhouettes and exaggerated squash-and-stretch on the mob. Lean hard into the brand palette: royal purple (#5824F5) dominates the hero mob and background gradients, electric cyan (#00A3FF) powers the cannon charge VFX and enemy base, and warm yellow (#FFD12C) is reserved exclusively for high-attention beats — the multiplier gate, coin explosions, and CTA. High-contrast rim lighting, punchy cel-shading, and a slight wide-angle lens to make crowds feel massive. Camera language borrows the archetype's "charge and release" energy: tight close-up → whip-pan → wide hero shot of the swarming army.
brief.rationale: We keep the winning archetype's "power-up and charge" emotional beat but rebuild it natively around Mob Control's actual core loop — aim, shoot, multiply, overrun — so the ad is honest and IPM-safe. The hero moment is the x10 gate transformation, which doubles as gameplay tutorial and dopamine payoff in under 7 seconds. Run as primary video creative for male 18-35 hyper-casual lookalikes; expect strong CTR from the satisfaction beat and improved D1 retention vs. bait-and-switch creatives.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, Stylized 3D Cartoon: a massive roaring purple mob of chunky cartoon stick-figures bursting through a giant glowing golden multiplier gate that reads 'x10!', cyan energy trails streaking behind them, royal purple #5824F5 dominant background with electric cyan #00A3FF rim light and warm yellow #FFD12C gate glow, dramatic low-angle wide lens, cel-shaded, high contrast, mobile game ad key art, on-screen text 'x10!' in bold yellow

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Stylized 3D Cartoon: extreme close-up on a cartoon cannon charging up with swirling cyan #00A3FF energy, a tiny nervous purple #5824F5 mob of 5 stick-figures clustered at its base, warm yellow #FFD12C sparks flying, deep purple gradient sky, player's finger silhouette dragging an aim line, punchy cel-shading, on-screen text 'AIM THE CANNON' in white with yellow outline

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Stylized 3D Cartoon: epic wide shot of an enormous purple #5824F5 cartoon mob flooding and smashing a cyan #00A3FF enemy fortress, yellow #FFD12C coins and confetti exploding skyward, motion blur and impact dust, vibrant high-contrast palette, energetic competitive mood, on-screen text 'MOB UNLEASHED' in chunky bold yellow letters

brief.title: Gate Stack Frenzy: Oddly Satisfying Mob Multiplier
brief.hook_3s: A single blue stickman fires through a glowing ×5 gate and EXPLODES into a stampeding purple crowd — all in 3 seconds.
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Top-down cannon POV fires one tiny stickman through a neon ×5 multiplier gate; crowd erupts into a satisfying wave with ASMR pop-pop-pop SFX.
3-7s ESCALATION: Finger drags cannon left/right, threading mob through stacked ×2, ×3, ×10 gates — counter spins from 5 → 47 → 312 with chunky number pops.
7-11s TENSION: Mob smashes into a rival red base; quick clash, purple crowd overwhelms it; coins and gems geyser out in yellow accent burst.
11-14s REWARD: Quick cut to base upgrade screen — a unit card levels up with golden shimmer, cannon visibly grows bigger.
14-15s CTA: End card with stampeding crowd silhouette behind bold logo + Play Now button pulsing in yellow.
brief.visual_direction: Stylized 3D Cartoon with chunky, low-poly characters and exaggerated squash-and-stretch on the mob. Dominant royal purple (#5824F5) battlefield floor with cyan (#00A3FF) glowing multiplier gates and yellow (#FFD12C) numbers/coins for maximum pop. High-contrast rim lighting on the crowd, slight motion blur on cannon shots, and tactile particle bursts at every gate hit. Camera stays in a slightly tilted top-down 'tabletop' angle for clarity, snap-zooming on the multiplier counter for satisfaction beats. UI mood is loud, kinetic, competitive — every action triggers a juicy SFX and number-pop.
brief.rationale: We port only the 'oddly satisfying' emotional beat from the winning archetype and re-skin it onto Mob Control's signature multiplier-gate-to-crowd-explosion moment, eliminating the install-to-bounce risk of a literal jigsaw hook. The royal purple + cyan gates + yellow number-pop combo locks visual brand recall in under 3s and matches the 18-35 male action audience expectation of momentum and scale. Test this against a vanilla gameplay capture; expect higher IPM via the satisfaction loop while preserving truthful gameplay representation.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, stylized 3D cartoon mobile game shot, top-down tilted view of a glowing cyan #00A3FF multiplier gate marked '×10' with a massive royal purple #5824F5 crowd of chunky low-poly stickmen stampeding through it, yellow #FFD12C number '×10 COMBO!' popping in bold cartoon font above the gate, particle sparks and rim lighting, vibrant high-contrast palette (#5824F5, #00A3FF, #FFD12C), energetic competitive mood, clean mobile-ad composition.

Storyboard frame 1, aspect 9:16, stylized 3D cartoon, close-up of a cartoon cannon firing a single blue stickman toward a row of glowing cyan #00A3FF multiplier gates on a royal purple #5824F5 battlefield, bold yellow #FFD12C overlay text 'HIT THE GATES →' at top, motion blur trails, palette (#5824F5, #00A3FF, #FFD12C), playful action mood.
